response.justification: Candidate is an experienced senior engineer with strong polyglot background (DLang, TypeScript, Node.js, Java, C#) and demonstrated ability to write low-latency systems at Sociomantic. However, the role specifically requires modern C++ or Rust expertise, and the resume shows only a brief C/C++ stint at National Instruments over a decade ago (2014-2015) with no recent C++ work. No geospatial routing or navigation tile pipeline experience, though GISCollective shows general GIS familiarity. Worth a screening call to assess C++ depth and willingness to ramp, but not a direct match.
strengths: Low-latency distributed systems experience at Sociomantic (millions of HTTP req/sec)
GIS/geospatial background via GISCollective and Green Map projects
Senior-level breadth across systems languages and OS-level concepts
concerns: No modern C++ or Rust experience shown; last C/C++ role was 2014-2015
No routing, tiling, or navigation pipeline domain experience
Primary recent stack is TypeScript/Node.js, far from the role's core requirements
